texts.orig: World consumption of natural gas at industrial plants increases in average by 1.7% yearly, and natural gas consumption in electroenergetics increases from 2012 till 2040 by 2.2% yearly [1 -3]. Meanwhile the fractional content of natural gas produced worldwide changes due to the transition of global gas industry to deeper deposits, as well as the active development of so-called ' shale gas ' in the USA, for which multicomponent composition is typical. During the development of such fi elds the volume of ' fat gas ' will increase signi fi cantly [1 -3]. In this connection, С 3+ hydrocarbon recovery from natural gas is a signi fi cant stage of gas stream preparation to transportation [4 -6]. texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this study, three di ff erent silicone rubbers with alkyl side groups were synthesized by using the recently proposed in-situ technique that combines two hydrosilylation reactions: the introduction of side alkyl group followed by polymer chains cross-linking in the same reaction mixture. The membranes can be obtained in one step by using commercially available materials such as polymethylhydrosiloxane as well as corresponded α -ole fi n and diole fi n. The completeness of polyalkylmethylsiloxane modi fi cation was con fi rmed by FTIR spectroscopy. As a result, dense membranes made of polyhexylmethylsiloxane (PHexMS), polyoctylmethylsiloxane (POMS) or polydecylmethylsiloxane (PDecMS) were fabricated for further investigation of their mechanical, structural and gas transport properties. In this work membranes from PDecMS were obtained and their gas transport properties were studied for the fi rst time. For the fi rst time, PHexMS and PDecMS were studied for their applicability in the separation of С 3+ hydrocarbons from methane. Among studied membrane materials, PDecMS demonstrates the highest selectivity for n-C4H10/CH4 gas pair separation ( α =27). An attempt was made in this work to explain from the point of view of the relationship structure-property the in fl uence of side chain substituent in polyalkylmethylsiloxanes on gas permeabilities and selectivities of membrane materials based on them.

The membranes can be obtained in one step by using commercially available materials such as polymethylhydrosiloxane as well as corresponded α -ole fi n and diole fi n. The completeness of polyalkylmethylsiloxane modi fi cation was con fi rmed by FTIR spectroscopy. As a result, dense membranes made of polyhexylmethylsiloxane (PHexMS), polyoctylmethylsiloxane (POMS) or polydecylmethylsiloxane (PDecMS) were fabricated for further investigation of their mechanical, structural and gas transport properties. In this work membranes from PDecMS were obtained and their gas transport properties were studied for the fi rst time. For the fi rst time, PHexMS and PDecMS were studied for their applicability in the separation of С 3+ hydrocarbons from methane. Among studied membrane materials, PDecMS demonstrates the highest selectivity for n-C4H10/CH4 gas pair separation ( α =27). An attempt was made in this work to explain from the point of view of the relationship structure-property the in fl uence of side chain substituent in polyalkylmethylsiloxanes on gas permeabilities and selectivities of membrane materials based on them.

texts.orig: High-permeable glassy polymers are characterized by high values of permeabilities and selectivities in hydrocarbons separation. However, permeances of such membranes decrease in time (e ff ect of ' physical aging ' ), which restrains their wide application in industry [19,20,40]. The studies being carried out in the fi eld of stabilization of polymer glasses properties may in perspective positively impact the commercialization of these materials in future [19,20]. For example, in some papers the crosslinking method was proposed to solve this problem [41,42].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Despite the variety of siloxane polymers, only two representatives are used in industrial membrane separation in the fi rst place: PDMS and POMS. Membranes based on PDMS possess high gas permeability due to high polymer chain mobility. Meanwhile, membranes based on POMS are characterized by higher values of separation selectivity as compared with PDMS [25]. It is accounted for the fact that with the increase of the length of hydrocarbon side chain substituent increases the a ffi nity of membrane material to hydrocarbons, and as a result, increases the sorption component of separation selectivity [29]. At the same time, the systematical study of the in fl uence of side group chemical structure in polyalkylmethylsiloxane on their С 3+ hydrocarbons and methane transport properties is scarce in the literature. Such studies are limited by the di ffi culty of synthesis of membrane materials based on polyalkylmethylsiloxane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We have recently proposed a new, one stage method to synthesis POMS membranes, which combines two reactions with the same Ptbased catalyst: the introduction of side group followed by polymer chains cross-linking [31]. It was allowed to use bulk chemicals for such polymer modi fi cations: 1-octen as a modifying component and 1,7-octadiene as a cross-linking agent (see Fig. 1C). It was found that the optimal ratio of cross-linking agent to modifying component is 5:95 [31]. The advantage of this in-situ technique is the fact that the side group substitution and cross-linking reactions take place simultaneously in one reaction mixture, and no further separation or puri fi cation is required. The aim of this study was to apply this in-situ technique to synthesize polyalkylmethylsiloxane with varied side group for further investigation of physical-mechanical properties of synthesized membrane materials and their gas transport properties for the separation of С 3+ hydrocarbon and methane. Also to study the in fl uence of alkyl substituent on permeabilities and selectivities of membrane materials based on them.

texts.orig: The solution of PMHS in n-hexane (3% wt.) was mixed with 1-alkene (modi fi cator), 30 μ l of Carsted catalyst was added. The solution was mixed at 60 °C for 2 h with back fl ow condenser. Afterward, 1,7octadiene (cross-linking agent) was added, PMHS content was reduced to the stoichiometric ratio, and the solution was mixed for 1 h at 60 °C. The membrane was cast on a stainless-steel net (mesh size 40 μ m) which was fi xed on a Te fl on surface with subsequent drying at 60 °C for 24 h. The residual solvent was removed by drying in the vacuum chamber. As modi fi cation agents 1-octene, 1-hexene, and 1-decene were used in this work, reference designations of the obtained membranes are listed in Table 1.

Solutions of vinyl terminated PDMS (3% wt. in toluene), cross-linking agent PMHS (1.5% wt. in toluene) and Carsted catalyst were prepared in separate fl asks. PMHS and Carsted catalyst solutions were added to PDMS solution, and the resulting solution was mixed for 1 h at 60 °C (PDMS:PMHS:cat ratio was 10:1:0.01). texts.text: For comparison, PDMS membranes were obtained. Solutions of vinyl terminated PDMS (3% wt. in toluene), cross-linking agent PMHS (1.5% wt. in toluene) and Carsted catalyst were prepared in separate fl asks. PMHS and Carsted catalyst solutions were added to PDMS solution, and the resulting solution was mixed for 1 h at 60 °C (PDMS:PMHS:cat ratio was 10:1:0.01). The resulting homogeneous solution was cast on a stainless steel net (mesh size 40 μ m) which was fi xed on a Te fl on surface with subsequent drying at 60 °C for 24 h. The residual solvent was removed by drying in the vacuum chamber. The same technique was used to obtain PDMS membrane samples without a net.

texts.orig: To determine sorption and swelling of polymer samples in toluene dense fi lms with a thickness not < 100 μ m were used. Mass and geometrical dimensions of fi lms specimens were determined before and after toluene exposure for 72 h [45]. Before the measurement excess toluene was gently removed from the surface with fi lter paper. Swelling equilibrium was achieved when the swollen samples reached a constant weight. To this end polymer samples were weighed every 8 h and afterward placed in toluene again. During the measurement of toluene sorption, we found out that after 72 h of experiment no weight change was observed for all studied specimen. Therefore we conclude that the polymer-solvent equilibrium was reached during this period of time. Crosslink density ( γ e) of the obtained membrane samples was calculated using Flory [45 -47] using data obtained from specimens toluene swelling:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Gas permeabilities and di ff usion coe ffi cients of membranes for nitrogen, methane, and propane were measured at 30° С according to Daynes-Barrer technique using the precise unit ' Helmholtz-Zentrum Geesthacht ' mounted with a pressure sensor ( ' Baratron ' ), accuracy 10 -7 bar. Permeability is given in Barrer. Sorption coe ffi cient was calculated as permeability ratio to di ff usion coe ffi cient. Membranes gas permeability was determined in the same way in work Borisov I.L. et al. [24]. Ideal selectivity ( α P) was calculated as a ratio of permeabilities of fast and slow permeating components. To measure gas permeabilities dense membranes were obtained, reinforced with a stainless steel net because materials based on siloxane polymers possess high elasticity and low tensile strength [48,49]. From Fig. 2 it is seen that polymer layer thickness exceeds the thickness of stainless steel, which allowed obtaining of dense membranes with a geometry close to the polymer fi lm. The application of such type membranes signi fi cantly simpli fi es the process of gas transport characteristics measurement and allows obtaining defect-free fi lms of less thickness. To investigate the in fl uence of stainless steel net on membrane transport characteristics dense and reinforced PDMS fi lms were studied, as of the material with well-known membrane properties. Values of methane and propane permeabilities and ideal permselectivity for dense and reinforced PDMS fi lms are given in Table 2. It is shown that the introduction of reinforcing net has a weak in fl uence on gas permeability values, while ideal permselectivity C3H8/CH4 and n-C4H10/CH4 did not change. A small reduction of permeabilities (> 10%) in the presence of reinforcing net can be explained by additional di ff usion resistance of gas molecules, connected with the introduction of the barrier material in the polymer matrix. At the same time, the obtained values of PDMS permeabilities and ideal permselectivities are in good agreement with literature data -1200-

texts.text: 3.1. Synthesis of polyalkylmethylsiloxane and their properties

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this work, an attempt was performed of one stage synthesis of materials based on polyalkylmethylsiloxane with di ff erent length of side-chain alkyl substituent. To this e ff ect, hydrosilylation reaction was performed (Fig. 1 c) between PMHS, linear α -ole fi ns ( С 6, С 8 or С 10) and 1,7-octadiene as a cross-linking agent. The ratio of cross-linking agent to α -ole fi n was 5/95% mol. To con fi rm the completeness of the hydrosilylation reaction all polymer specimens were studied by IR-spectroscopy. As it seen from Fig. 3, in spectra of substituted PHexMS, POMS and PDecMS specimens is observed complete absence of Si e H bonds (2168 cm -1 ) and double bonds from α -ole fi ns, which is indicated by the absence of spectral bands higher than 3000 с m -1 from = СН 2, 1640 -60 cm -1 from С = С and 910 с m -1 from deformation vibrations in СН = СН 2 junction. At the same time saturated СН 2- groups are observed in all spectra of specimens (bands 2840 -2960 cm -1 -ν CH, 1380 -1460 cm -1 -δ CH). In comparison with the initial PHMS, for the substituted specimens bands from Si e O (1000 -1100 cm -1 ) and Si e C (900 -800 с m -1 ) bonds are distinctly shifted in the region of long waves which is a direct indication of new Si e C bonds formation. Thus, based on IR-spectroscopy data is shown the quantitative occurrence of hydrosilylation reaction for all the studied polyalkylmethylsiloxane.

texts.orig: At room temperature polyalkylmethylsiloxanes are in a rubbery state which is typical for rubbers. It is indicated by a high value of glasstransition temperature Tg (Fig.4). It is worth noting that with an increase of hydrocarbon side-chain substituent length from С 6 to С 10 an increase in Tg from -105.1 to -68.3° С is observed (Fig. 4). In work Stern S.A. et al. [29] methyl, ethyl, propyl and octyl substituents where introduced into polyalkylmethylsiloxane, meanwhile, Tg increased from -123° С to -92° С (Fig. 4). For these materials, Stern S.A. et al. [29] connect the increase in glass-transition temperature of the polymer upon the introduction of bulkier side-chain substituent with an increase of chain sti ff ness, and thereby explain the decrease in di ff usion coe ffi cients. Glass-transition temperature of POMS (Tg = -93° С ) in the current work corresponds well with Tg = -92° С of POMS [29], and Tg of PHexMS ( -105.1° С ) with glass-transition temperature cited in work Villegas J.A. and Cervantes J [53].: -108° С . On Fig. 4 a distinct correlation is observed of an increase in glass-transition temperature with an increase in hydrocarbon substituent length. An exception is PDMS, which glass-transition temperature is higher than that of polyethylmethylsiloxane. This fact is caused by the speci fi city of polyethylmethylsiloxane as a membrane material [54].

texts.orig: Distinctive minimums on thermograms (Fig. 5) indicate a phase transition at temperatures close to Tg of the polymer material. It might be a result of partially arranged structures in the amorphous polymer which deteriorate with a temperature increase. Most distinctively this e ff ect is observed for the membrane material based on PDecMS (Fig. 5). With an increase in the length of the hydrocarbon radical in two methyl groups in a row С 6 -С 8 -С 10 thermal e ff ect of such kind of transition increases in the order of magnitude: 2.9 -38.4 -268.4 mJ correspondingly. Similar DSC relationships are observed for other comb -like polymers (polymers with alkyl radicals CnH2n+1 as side branches) [55]. For instance in works Kaufman H.S. et al. [59], Robello D.R [60]. authors notice a similar transition for polyacrylates, which indicates the formation of the liquid-crystal structure due to the arrangement of sidechain substituents at temperatures below glass-transition temperature.

texts.orig: characteristic for cross-linked PDMS and indicates cross-links between molecules. Thus, it can be concluded that for polyalkylmethylsiloxane samples with an equal concentration of the cross-linking agent diffraction peak near 10° indicates intermolecular cross-links (Fig. 6 a). At liquid nitrogen temperature ( i.e. in the temperature region below glasstransition temperature of polyalkylmethylsiloxane) a shift and strati fi cation of this peak are observed for polyalkylmethylsiloxane (Fig. 6 b). This e ff ect may relate to a decrease in mobility of macromolecule fragments. At such conditions, interplanar spacings which are connected with the relative arrangement of polymer chains due to crosslinking and relative orientation of side-chain substituents, as well as interplanar spacings between aliphatic side chains become more clearly exhibited. Di ff raction peak in the region of 20° corresponds to the interplanar spacing of 4.5 Å and is a consequence of the arrangement of side-chain PMS substituents. According to data published in work Plate N.A. and Shibaev V.P. [55] such interplanar spacing is characteristic for parallel packing of n-para ffi nes, moreover if the number of carbon atoms exceeds 6, packing close to hexagonal is ful fi lled. Authors [55] note that in case of comb -shaped polymers in amorphous state packing of side substituents to a large extent is similar to the hexagonal packing of low-molecular n-para ffi nes. During the transition from room temperature (Fig. 6 а ) to liquid nitrogen temperature (Fig. 6b) peak in the region of 20° shifts to 21 -22° ( i.e. , the interplanar spacing decreases to 4.1 -4.0 Å). The peak on the di ff raction pattern becomes clearer which may indicate compaction of meso-phase structure of side substituents due to the decrease of their mobility at low temperature. Such arrangement of aliphatic fragments (interplanar spacing from 4.5 to 4.1 -4.0 Å) may lead to thermal e ff ects in the region of glass-transition temperature presented in Fig. 5.

The membrane materials characterization using individual gas permeabilities is a reliable and widely spread method which allows comparing materials, obtained by di ff erent research groups [56 -58]. texts.orig: The increase in the size of side-chain substituent from С 1 to С 10 results in the decrease of gas di ff usion coe ffi cients (DC4H10 =6.3·10 -6 , 3.5·10 -6 , 3.4·10 -6 and 2.7·10 -6 с m 2 /s, correspondingly). Such decrease of di ff usion coe ffi cients is induced by a reduction of chain fl exibility, which corresponds with the tendency of glass-transition temperature to rise with the increase of alkyl substituent length (Fig. 4). Besides, this e ff ect is observed not only for bulky molecules (n-butane), but also for molecules with comparatively small e ff ective diameter (carbon dioxide): the di ff usion coe ffi cient decreases with the increase in side chain substituent length (D СО 2 =2.4·10 -6 , 1.4·10 -6 , 1.3·10 -6 and 1.0·10 -6 с m 2 /s, correspondingly). An explanation of this e ff ect if presented in work Lee C.L. et al. [54] based on the theory of free volume. Authors showed that with the increase of glass-transition temperature in the row of polyalkylmethylsiloxanes free volume fraction of polymer decreases and as a result permeabilities and di ff usion coe ffi cients decrease. An exception to this rule is the only polymethylethylesiloxane [54].

texts.orig: A signi fi cant feature of studied materials is the increase of di ff usion selectivity (n-C4H10/CH4) with the increase of hydrocarbon substituent length from 0.25 for PDMS to 0.33 for PDecMS (Fig. 8). This is not a trivial fact because as a rule, the decrease in membrane material gas permeability results in the increase of di ff usion selectivity for penetrant with smaller molecule size. Such an e ff ect may be connected with features of the microstructure of polyalkylmethylsiloxanes with bulky microstructure caused by a character of supramolecular organization of such polymers. This conforms to WAXS data (Fig. 6) which indicate the appearance of additional ordered structural elements in the polymer with the increase in the length of hydrocarbon substituent.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this work, the dense membranes made of polyhexylmethylsiloxane (PHexMS), polyoctylmethylsiloxane (POMS) and polydecylmethylsiloxane (PDecMS) were fabricated by using one-stage synthesis method based on hydrosilylation reaction of polymethylsiloxane with linear α -ole fi ns ( С 6, С 8 or С 10) and 1,7-octadiene as a cross-linking agent. In this work for the fi rst time were obtained membranes based on PDecMS and their gas permeation properties were studied. Also for the fi rst time are reported CO2 and hydrocarbons transport properties of PHexMS and PDecMS. The completeness of these reactions for all polymers was con fi rmed by FTIR-spectroscopy. The increase of side chain length from C6 to C10 was accompanied with the decrease in the polymer chain fl exibility resulted in the change of glass transition temperature Tg from -123° С to -92° С .
texts.text: In this work, the dense membranes made of polyhexylmethylsiloxane (PHexMS), polyoctylmethylsiloxane (POMS) and polydecylmethylsiloxane (PDecMS) were fabricated by using one-stage synthesis method based on hydrosilylation reaction of polymethylsiloxane with linear α -ole fi ns ( С 6, С 8 or С 10) and 1,7-octadiene as a cross-linking agent. In this work for the fi rst time were obtained membranes based on PDecMS and their gas permeation properties were studied. Also for the fi rst time are reported CO2 and hydrocarbons transport properties of PHexMS and PDecMS. The completeness of these reactions for all polymers was con fi rmed by FTIR-spectroscopy. The increase of side chain length from C6 to C10 was accompanied with the decrease in the polymer chain fl exibility resulted in the change of glass transition temperature Tg from -123° С to -92° С .

Such arrangement of aliphatic fragments may lead to thermal e ff ects in the region of glass-transition temperature.
